title: Say Cheese for National Cheese Lovers Day!
story: Every January 20, cheese fans across America celebrate National Cheese Lovers Day by indulging in their favorite dairy delight! People host cheese-tasting parties, create epic grilled cheese sandwiches, and share photos of their cheesiest meals on social media. Restaurants offer special cheese-focused menus, while grocery stores display fancy cheese platters. Some families have fondue nights, dipping everything from bread to fruit into melted cheese. Pizza places see extra business as people order their cheesiest pies. Whether you love sharp cheddar, creamy brie, or stretchy mozzarella, this delicious holiday gives everyone permission to go completely cheese-crazy for an entire day!
activity_question: What do some families enjoy doing on National Cheese Lovers Day?
activity_choices: Having fondue nights with melted cheese
activity_answer: Having fondue nights with melted cheese
bonus_fact: Americans eat about 33 pounds of cheese per person every year!

title: Spin the Beat: National DJ Day
story: DJs across the country drop their hottest beats on January 20 for National DJ Day! Radio stations host special mix shows, nightclub DJs share behind-the-scenes videos on social media, and music lovers celebrate the artists who keep us dancing. Many DJs give shoutouts to their fans, play listener requests all day long, and some even teach kids how to mix music using simple apps and equipment.
activity_question: What do many DJs do on National DJ Day to connect with their fans?
activity_choices: Give shoutouts and play listener requests
activity_answer: Give shoutouts and play listener requests
follow_up_question: What kind of music would you want to learn to mix if you were a DJ?

title: Crunch Time for Candy Lovers!
story: Every January 20, candy lovers across America celebrate National Buttercrunch Day by indulging in this sweet, crunchy treat! Buttercrunch is a delicious toffee candy covered in chocolate and often topped with chopped nuts. People mark this special day by visiting candy shops, making homemade buttercrunch in their kitchens, or sharing their favorite store-bought varieties with friends and family. Many bakeries and confectioneries offer special buttercrunch deals and samples to celebrate. Social media fills with photos of golden, crunchy squares as people show off their buttercrunch creations and discoveries. It's the perfect excuse to satisfy your sweet tooth!
activity_question: What is buttercrunch candy typically covered with?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite crunchy candy or snack to enjoy?

title: Waddle You Do for Penguin Awareness Day?
story: Every January 20, people around the world celebrate National Penguin Awareness Day by learning about these amazing tuxedo-wearing birds! Zoos host special penguin talks and feeding shows, while schools organize fun penguin facts contests. Many families watch penguin documentaries together or visit aquariums to see these flippered friends up close. Social media fills with adorable penguin videos and photos as people share their favorite penguin moments. Some kids even dress up like penguins or waddle around their houses pretending to slide on ice! It's the perfect day to discover why penguins are such incredible swimmers and caring parents.
activity_question: What do many families do together on National Penguin Awareness Day?
bonus_fact: Emperor penguins can dive deeper than 1,800 feet underwater!

title: Step Outside for Fresh Air Fun!
story: Every January 20, people celebrate National Take a Walk Outdoors Day by doing exactly what it sounds like - taking walks outside! Families bundle up in coats and scarves to explore their neighborhoods, hike forest trails, or stroll through parks. Some people walk their dogs, others hunt for cool rocks or interesting leaves. Many families make it a fun adventure by bringing cameras to snap photos of birds, trees, or funny-shaped clouds they spot along the way.
activity_question: What do some families bring on their outdoor walks to capture memories?
follow_up_question: What's the most interesting thing you've ever discovered while walking outside?

title: Crunch Time for Granola Bars!
story: Every January 20, people across America celebrate National Granola Bar Day by munching on their favorite chewy or crunchy bars! Some folks bake homemade granola bars with oats, nuts, and honey, while others stock up on store-bought varieties. Schools often serve granola bars as healthy snacks, and hikers pack them for trail energy. It's the perfect excuse to try new flavors!
activity_question: What are two main textures that granola bars can have?
follow_up_question: What ingredients would you put in your dream granola bar?
